mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 50%, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative forward returns in over 55% of cases, or the regime filter shows no predictive alpha compared to unconditional signals.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches 210.0, or the threshold shows no out-of-sample predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates creates a favorable growth-and-liquidity environment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising GDPC1 and falling FEDFUNDS yields negative forward returns in over 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that outweighs labor market stability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative returns with a win rate above 50% during recessions, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward testing.
